en.extract:
The golden babbler (Cyanoderma chrysaeum) is a babbler species in the family Timaliidae. It occurs from the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas to Southeast Asia and inhabits subtropical lowland and montane forests. It is listed as Least Concern on the IUCN Red List because of its wide distribution.
It has olive-green wings and yellow underparts. Its crown and nape are golden-yellow with narrow stripes. It is 19–12 cm (7.5–4.7 in) long and weighs 6–10 g (0.21–0.35 oz).
Stachyris chrysaea was the scientific name proposed by Edward Blyth in 1844 who described an olivaceous babbler with a yellow crown from Nepal.
Since 2016, it is recognised as a Cyanoderma species.


References


External links
BirdLife International (2019). "Golden Babbler Cyanoderma chrysaeum".

The '''golden babbler''' ('''''Cyanoderma chrysaeum''''') is a babbler [[species]] in the family [[Timaliidae]]. It occurs from the foothills of the [[Eastern Himalayas]] to [[Southeast Asia]] and inhabits subtropical lowland and [[montane forest]]s. It is listed as [[Least Concern]] on the [[IUCN Red List]] because of its wide distribution.<ref name="iucn status 15 November 2021" />

It has olive-green wings and yellow underparts. Its crown and nape are golden-yellow with narrow stripes. It is {{convert|19-12|cm|abbr=on}} long and weighs {{convert|6-10|g|abbr=on}}.<ref name=HBoW2016>{{cite book |year=2016 |editor1=del Hoyo, J. |editor2=Elliott, A. |editor3=Sargatal, J. |editor4=Christie, D. A. |editor5=de Juana, E. |title=Handbook of the Birds of the World |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona |volume=2: Passerines |last1=Collar |first1=N. J. |last2=Robson |first2=C. |chapter=Golden Babbler (''Cyanoderma chrysaeum'') |doi=10.2173/bow.golbab1.01 |chapter-url=https://www.hbw.com/species/golden-babbler-cyanoderma-chrysaeum }}</ref>

''Stachyris chrysaea'' was the scientific name proposed by [[Edward Blyth]] in 1844 who [[scientific description|described]] an olivaceous babbler with a yellow crown from Nepal.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Blyth |first1=E. |year=1844 |title=Appendix for Mr. Blyth's report for December Meeting 1842 |journal=The Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal |volume=13 |issue=149 |pages=361–395 |url=https://archive.org/details/journalofasiatic131asia/page/379}}</ref>
Since 2016, it is recognised as a ''[[Cyanoderma]]'' species.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Moyle |first1=R. G. |last2=Andersen |first2=M. J. |last3=Oliveros |first3=C. H. |last4=Steinheimer |first4=F. D. |last5=Reddy |first5=S. |year=2012 |title=Phylogeny and Biogeography of the Core Babblers (Aves: Timaliidae) |journal=Systematic Biology |volume=61 |issue=4 |pages=631–651 |doi=10.1093/sysbio/sys027 |pmid=22328569|doi-access=free }}</ref><ref name=HBoW2016/>

zh.extract:
金头穗鹛（学名：Cyanoderma chrysaeum），是画眉科的一种，分布于老挝、孟加拉国、不丹、中国大陆、越南、印度、泰国、印度尼西亚、缅甸、马来西亚、尼泊尔和柬埔寨。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
金头穗鹛的平均体重约为7.8克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的（低地）湿润疏灌丛、亚热带或热带严重退化的前森林、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带的高海拔疏灌丛和亚热带或热带的湿润山地林。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。


亚种
金头穗鹛滇南亚种（学名：Cyanoderma chrysaeum aurata）。分布于缅甸、老挝、越南以及中国大陆的云南等地。该物种的模式产地在泰国DoiPhaHomPok。
金头穗鹛指名亚种（学名：Cyanoderma chrysaeum chrydaea）。分布于尼泊尔、锡金、不丹、印度、缅甸以及中国大陆的云南、西藏等地。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。
